issues_classified.description: Post-hoc criterion revision for H_Mech: Original pass criteria failed on 14/15 runs (93.3%); criteria revised to encoder effect > 0.5 and decoder effect < 0.1 after observing data. Not pre-registered; paper acknowledges exploratory status. Destroys confirmatory status of primary empirical claim.
issues_classified.suggestion: Pre-register revised criteria in OSF before running full experiment, OR reframe H_Mech as exploratory pilot-scale evidence and conduct new experiment with revised criteria as official stopping rule. Estimated effort: 2-3 hours for pre-registration + new experiment run.

issues_classified.description: Three different absorption metrics used across experiments without established equivalence: factorial decomposition uses 'overlap fraction', multi-seed validation uses 'Jaccard overlap', and safety analysis uses 'cosine-based proportional absorption'. Table 1 presents all three as comparable, preventing legitimate cross-experiment comparisons.
issues_classified.suggestion: Pick one canonical metric (recommend Jaccard overlap as most interpretable) and recompute all experiments. Alternatively, split Table 1 into metric-specific sections and remove all cross-experiment numerical comparisons. Estimated effort: 4-6 hours.

issues_classified.description: H3 steering numbers inconsistency: Abstract and Section 4.3 report ratio=0.776, p=0.273; experiment_summary.json reports ratio=0.974, p=0.936. Irreconcilable mismatch.
issues_classified.suggestion: Trace the H3 steering experiment to determine correct numbers from raw data. Update paper consistently. Verify Figure 3 reflects correct data. Estimated effort: 2-3 hours investigation + updates.

issues_classified.description: Random SAE baselines show zero variance (std ~0 across all 5 seeds in multiseed_validation.json). Cohen's d > 10 is mathematically misleading when one distribution has zero variance.
issues_classified.suggestion: Investigate why random SAEs produce zero-variance absorption (measurement artifact vs genuine determinism). Recalculate effect sizes with appropriate variance estimates. If genuine, use a different baseline (e.g., trained SAE with shuffled labels) that provides non-zero variance comparison. Estimated effort: 3-4 hours.

issues_classified.description: Held-out generalization correlation (r=0.998) based on only 5 data points (n=1 held-out hierarchy per seed). With 3 degrees of freedom, confidence interval could be wide. Paper presents as 'perfect generalization' without CI.
issues_classified.suggestion: Increase to n=3-5 held-out hierarchies per seed for reliable correlation estimate. Report CI explicitly for r=0.998 claim. Estimated effort: 4-6 hours for additional experiments.

issues_classified.description: Safety analysis saturates at ~97% absorption for both safety and non-safety groups (0.967 vs 0.968). At 97%, no dynamic range to detect differences. Mann-Whitney p=0.989 null result is uninterpretable.
issues_classified.suggestion: Use different absorption metric with better dynamic range. Alternatively, analyze whether the 3% non-absorbed features are systematically non-safety features. Estimated effort: 2-3 hours to re-analyze with different metric.

issues_classified.description: Section 3.5 promises one-way ANOVA across all completed variants in Results but never reports it. Pairwise effect sizes used instead (more appropriate given low power), but unfulfilled promise undermines credibility.
issues_classified.suggestion: Either add ANOVA to Results section with appropriate caveats about low power (n=3 variants, 5 replicates, df=2,12), or remove the promise from Section 3.5 methodology. Estimated effort: 1-2 hours.

issues_classified.description: Capacity-pressure mechanism (lower L0 -> higher absorption) is post-hoc. Paper does not distinguish between capacity-pressure absorption vs TopK artifact (sparser activation patterns incidentally resembling absorption).
issues_classified.suggestion: Add diagnostic test: compare with JumpReLU SAE at equivalent L0. If L0 relationship only holds for TopK, mechanism interpretation changes. Estimated effort: 4-5 hours.

issues_classified.description: All synthetic experiments at d_model=128, far below real LLM dimensions (768 to 12,288). Encoder-decoder dynamics could fundamentally change at scale. No scaling validation provided.
issues_classified.suggestion: Add d_model=512 validation point to establish whether encoder dominance scales. Explicitly acknowledge d_model=128 as pilot-scale choice. Estimated effort: 6-8 hours.

issues_classified.description: Paper claims 'first factorial decomposition' and 'encoder dominance' novelty, but Oursland (2026) theoretically derives encoder-decoder asymmetry and SAEBench already compares architectures noting sparsity differences. Actual novelty is empirical quantification, not discovery.
issues_classified.suggestion: Restate contribution as 'first empirical quantification of encoder-decoder asymmetry via controlled factorial decomposition' to avoid overclaiming. Estimated effort: 30 minutes.

issues_classified.description: Figure 9 referenced in figure list but never cited in text (figure_9_summary_table.pdf). Creates confusion about whether supplementary or missing.
issues_classified.suggestion: Either cite Figure 9 in appropriate Results section or remove it from figure list. Estimated effort: 15 minutes.

issues_fixed: Multi-seed validation now shows non-zero within-seed variance (0.02-0.08 per seed) addressing the zero-variance concern from iteration 1
Paper now explicitly discusses five limitations including synthetic-only scope and post-hoc criterion revision
Factorial decomposition properly executed with 2x2 design crossing trained/random encoder and decoder
success_patterns: Honest negative results: H3 steering and H_Safe negative results reported without spin, with specific p-values and effect sizes
Factorial decomposition method is genuinely novel as a measurement technique for isolating encoder vs decoder contributions
Clear contribution framing with six enumerated contributions
Efficient experiment execution: all experiments completed well under time estimates
systemic_patterns: Post-hoc revision pattern: iteration 1 had zero-variance issues, iteration 2 has H_Mech criterion revision - indicates need for tighter pre-registration discipline
Metric inconsistency across experiments (3 different absorption metrics) is a fundamental measurement issue appearing in both iterations
Synthetic-only scope at d_model=128 limits real-world generalization claims in both iterations

suggestions: Experiments are efficient - no GPU scheduling improvements needed
Focus improvement effort on writing consistency (H3 numbers mismatch) and pre-registration rigor
Consider running d_model=512 validation in parallel with writing tasks to save iteration time

recommended_focus: Pre-register H_Mech revised criteria in OSF before next experiment run
Resolve H3 steering numbers inconsistency by tracing correct values from raw data
Add d_model=512 validation to establish scaling behavior
Standardize on one absorption metric (recommend Jaccard overlap) and recompute all experiments
Add JumpReLU comparison to test capacity-pressure vs TopK artifact hypothesis
